{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tenri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[Tenri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middle[[Category:middle]] tiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tenri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[Tenri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middle[[Category:middle]] tiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tenri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[Tenri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middle[[Category:middle]] tiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tenri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[Tenri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middle[[Category:middle]] tiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[Tenri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middle[[Category:middle]] tiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tenri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[Tenri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middle[[Category:middle]] tiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tenri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[Tenri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middle[[Category:middle]] tiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middle[[Category:middle]] tier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[Tenri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middletiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middletier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>

For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middletiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middletier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tenri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[Tenri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middletiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middletier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tenri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[Tenri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middletiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middletier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tenri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[Tenri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middletiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middletier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middletiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middletier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Tenri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[Tenri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middletiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middletier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a stone coffin lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped stone coffin was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1407109047640.html Tenri City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://kanko-tenri.jp/tourist-spots/south/kushiyamakofun/ Tenri Tourist Information]{{in lang|ja}}
Kushiyama Kofun

For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middletiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. A piece of a Sarcophagus lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped Sarcophagus was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kushiyama Kofun]][[en:Kushiyama Kofun]][[ja:櫛山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11544664]] Wikidata name: Kushiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.556944|N|135.851944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11534332}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Q11410445|WD=Q11410445}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middletiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middletier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a Sarcophagus lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped Sarcophagus was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Yanagimoto Station|Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kushiyama Kofun]][[en:Kushiyama Kofun]][[ja:櫛山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11544664]] Wikidata name: Kushiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.556944|N|135.851944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11534332}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Q11410445|WD=Q11410445}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middletiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middletier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a Sarcophagus lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped Sarcophagus was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Yanagimoto Station|Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>

After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a Sarcophagus lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped Sarcophagus was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Yanagimoto Station|Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kushiyama Kofun]][[en:Kushiyama Kofun]][[ja:櫛山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11544664]] Wikidata name: Kushiyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Kushiyama Kofun | native_name =櫛山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kushiyama Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kushiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.556944|N|135.851944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11534332}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|Q11410445|WD=Q11410445}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|33|25.0|N|135|51|7.0|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Kushiyama Kofun'''|櫛山古墳|}} is an early [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Yanagimoto neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1957.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=hhttps://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210820|title=櫛山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref> Kushiyama Kofun is one of the Yanagimoto Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kushiyama Kofun is adjacent to the east side of Andonyama Kofun (the tomb of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]). Its shape is highly unusual in that it is a {{nihongo|''sōhōchūen-fun''|双方中円墳}}, with a central circular mound and short rectangular extensions on either side. For this tumulus, one of the sides is lost, creating a similarity to a typical {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however there is no narrowing or "neck" connecting the two portions. The tumulus is built up in three tiers, with the tops of the front and middletiers flat and scattered with white pebbles 3 - 4 centimeters in diameter, suggesting that they were laid out around the burial facility. It is believed that the entire mound was covered with slab-shaped ''[[fukiishi]]'' about 30 cm in size. An [[archaeological excavation]] was carried out in 1948 to preserve the tumulus, revealing that it was 148 meters long, with a diameter of 90 meters at the middletier, 60 meters in the length and width of the front tier, and 25 meters in the rear tier. A moat remains around the tumulus, measuring 190 meters east–west and 165 meters north–south, with the width of the moat on the side of the front tier being approximately 56 meters.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
In the center of the central circular portion, there is a vertical-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] perpendicular to the main axis, with an estimated total length of 7.1 meters, width of about 1.4 meters, and height of over 1.2 meters. This was excavated when the Japanese military constructed an airfield nearby in 1943. There was no burial facility at the rear mound of the tumulus, but there was a ritual facility. After the burials were completed, rituals were conducted out in which jasper bracelets, ''[[magatama]]'' and tubular beads, iron swords, knives and axes, and clay products imitating these and other everyday objects, were intentionally broken and thrown into a large excavated hole along with the stone blocks. A piece of a Sarcophagus lid with a rope hanging protrusion and a stone piece that is thought to be the bottom of the coffin from the end plate were excavated from inside the burial chamber, and it is assumed that a long-shaped Sarcophagus was placed in the depression in the center,<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Yanagimoto Station|Yanagimoto Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]]<ref name="Shiseki"/>
<gallery> Kusiyama kohun 001.jpg|Aerial view of Kushiyama Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
